exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a clear, sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout.
Uses concrete examples from both natural sciences and human sciences to illustrate the impact of knowledge on wonder.
Acknowledges an alternative perspective (e.g., the placebo effect) to show awareness of possible diminution of wonder.
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive, with limited TOK-style examination of methods, justification and reliability.
Links to areas of knowledge and ways of knowing are uneven and under-developed; comparisons of mythic versus scientific explanations lack critical depth.
Evaluation of counter-claims and implications is brief and insufficiently explored, particularly in the conclusion and discussion of broader implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total of 3 reflects an essay that, while focused and well-illustrated, falls short of developing the critical analysis and evaluative depth required at higher bands. Its strengths lie in coherent structure and relevant examples, but pervasive descriptive treatment, weak engagement with ways of knowing, and under-developed counter-perspectives limit its overall achievement. A band-3 level accurately captures the balance of clear focus against superficial TOK analysis, justifying the assignment of 3 marks.

annotations.comment: The transition into the natural sciences section lacks a clear topic sentence signalling the shift. Introduce explicit signposting to improve coherence and guide the reader through each area of knowledge.

The conclusion restates the thesis and examples but lacks reflection on the implications of the argument. To reach a higher band, explore how knowledge shapes wonder across other AOKs or how sustaining wonder might influence future research.

There is minimal discussion of ways of knowing such as perception, emotion, or reason. To enrich the analysis, explicitly explore how different WOKs contribute to or transform our sense of wonder in each AOK.

The citation of Waldek & Dobrijevic (2022) appears to be from secondary media. To strengthen credibility, engage with academic texts on atmospheric physics and discuss how expert consensus informs knowledge claims.

The student relies on general-interest articles (LeWine, Cherry) for the Placebo Effect. Reflect on how the choice of sources in human sciences affects the perceived reliability and depth of knowledge claims.

The essay references Cleveland Clinic (2023) alongside a mythological website. Contrast the epistemic authority of medical research versus mythological narratives and discuss how credibility impacts wonder.

The mythic descriptions of the aurora are juxtaposed with scientific explanations, yet the essay does not evaluate their respective capacities to foster wonder. Add a critical comparison of narrative versus empirical approaches.

The essay conflates correlation with causation in modern explanations of depression. Discuss how methodological rigorâsuch as control of variablesâaffects the reliability of these knowledge claims and their relation to wonder.
The conclusion misses an opportunity to reflect on broader implications for knowledge production itself. Propose how maintaining a sense of wonder might shape future research practices.
The opening generalisation about human curiosity is broad. Replace with a concise TOK framingâperhaps referencing a theory of wonderâto align more directly with assessment criteria.

The essay restates the prescribed title but lacks an explicit formulation of the knowledge question that will guide the analysis. Consider articulating the core knowledge question at the outset to clarify the line of inquiry.

The introduction defines wonder and outlines the essayâs perspective, but it remains largely descriptive. To strengthen the TOK focus, define key terms such as âknowledgeâ and âwonderâ via relevant WOKs and specify how each AOKâs methodology will be examined.

Good illustrative contrast with myth, but the essay does not evaluate the strengths and limitations of mythic versus scientific explanations for fostering wonder. Add critical comparison of narrative richness versus empirical complexity.

The detailed explanation of photosynthesis and cell division demonstrates rigour, but the TOK analysis is missing. Evaluate how empirical evidence and experimental methodology contribute to wonder differently than myth-based explanations.

While the scientific explanation of the aurora borealis is accurate, the student misses an opportunity to discuss how theoretical models in physics shape our sense of wonder. Reflect on whether simplified models limit or enhance awe compared to mythic narratives.

The discussion of potential loss of wonder if the Placebo Effect is fully explained is insightful. To extend this, evaluate whether familiarity with scientific explanations necessarily reduces emotional impact, drawing on psychological theories of habituation.

The student acknowledges ongoing uncertainty in explaining the Placebo Effectâthis is promising. However, they should discuss criteria for knowledge in human sciences, such as reproducibility and ethical considerations, to deepen the analysis.

The comparison of past and present conceptions of depression is effective, but the analysis stops short of evaluating the reliability of mental health knowledge claims. Discuss methodological limits of correlational studies and diagnostic criteria.

Although the aurora is a strong natural sciences example, each example needs an explicit tie back to the main title question at every stage to remind the reader how discovering the cause influences our sense of wonder.
The discussion of the placebo effect is descriptive; to deepen analysis, include a real-life study or statistical data on its efficacy, linking how scale and impact influence the sense of wonder.

The narrative of Oizys provides cultural perspective, but the student should analyse how the paradigm shift from myth to clinical models in human sciences changes the ways of knowing and affects the assessment of wonder.

The reference to Tilley & Ellis (2011) is from a popular source rather than primary literature. Use peer-reviewed studies to support your natural science examples and demonstrate awareness of academic reliability standards.

The treatment of photosynthesis lacks a counterargument. Introduce an alternative scientific perspective, such as emerging research on plant signalling, to show how wonder persists amid evolving knowledge.
The student could analyze ethical dimensions of placebo research design and how these influence the production and acceptance of knowledge, thereby affecting the sense of wonder.
The discussion assumes a uniform audience response to the loss of wonder. Incorporate perspectives from psychology on individual variation in experiencing awe to nuance the argument.

1â5 or is not a

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
